title: fraxinus excelsior var. heterophylla
intro: Fraxinus excelsior var. heterophylla, also known as the European ash with alternate leaves, is a variant of the Fraxinus excelsior tree species. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat fever and rheumatism due to its high concentration of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and support digestive health. Regular consumption of Fraxinus excelsior var. heterophylla has been associated with various health benefits, including improved cardiovascular health, reduced risk of certain cancers, and enhanced immune function.
